expected_trades_per_day_basis: Mirror archetype E148 (pricechange channel) had trig=988 over the ~30d window ≈33/day raw, but porting to OI_Price channel which has lower base frequency (~61 entries vs 60 share but OI_Price events are rarer per symbol) plus the dual filter (global_short_ratio_high AND oi_change_pct>=12 AND liquidity_bucket=high) should cut to ~5-8% of OI_Price events; estimating ~70-75 triggers/30d ≈ 2.4/day.

best_in: BTC choppy/range-bound days where alt OI builds without index trend
funding rate negative or rapidly turning negative on the symbol
post-crash recovery sessions (24-72h after a flush) when shorts crowd in
worst_in: strong sustained BTC downtrend where global short positioning is correct, not crowded
low-volatility weekends with thin OI changes (oi_change_pct>=12 rarely fires)
high-funding-positive regimes where shorts are already paying — squeeze fuel diluted
regime_sensitivity.explanation: This is a contrarian squeeze-fuel setup: it needs shorts to be wrong and trapped. It performs best when crowd positioning is asymmetric vs realized direction. It fails when shorts are correctly positioned in a real downtrend, because the OI burst is fresh shorting that gets paid, not squeezed.

live_readiness_caveats: global_short_ratio data has 5min-1h staleness on Binance public endpoints — by entry time the ratio may have already mean-reverted; track time-since-funding-update and skip entries where stale > 30min.
liquidity_bucket=high filter still admits mid-cap alts where a 12% OI jump is one whale — concentration risk; cap per-symbol exposure and track top-3-symbol share of trades.
OI_Price channel triggers can fire near-simultaneously with pricechange channel on the same symbol — risk of double-sizing the same thesis; add cross-channel dedup window of 5-10min.

monitoring_metrics.rationale: E148 sibling backtested +0.350 — paper median should stay positive; rolling 5-trade window detects regime shift before equity damage compounds.
If trigger count diverges from 2.4/day estimate, the global_short_ratio_high or oi_change_pct>=12 thresholds are mis-calibrated for current regime — strategy is silently off-distribution.
Devil flagged channel-semantics mismatch; if one whale account on one symbol drives signal, edge is illusory.
Synthesizer flagged funding-rate staleness — stale signal = squeeze thesis already priced in.
Long-side squeeze plays cluster their losses (failed squeezes happen in batches during real downtrends); 5+ consecutive flags regime mismatch.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling Sharpe < 0 AND trades_per_day actual is within predicted band
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Triggers firing as expected but losing means the squeeze-fuel thesis is invalidated for current market structure, not a sampling artifact.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: E126/E064 (sibling pricechange long entries) simultaneously degrade in paper-shadow
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: This archetype is a channel-port of the same squeeze-fuel logic; if the parent family fails, the port has no independent edge.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Realized max drawdown exceeds 8% on paper-shadow within first 30 trades
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: R2 lesson: E126 backtested +0.3946 compounded to -13.5% live due to asymmetric TP/SL tail — early DD>8% means we're seeing the same left-tail trap.

expected_max_dd_basis: Sibling E148 backtest score 0.350 with similar long-squeeze profile; assuming Round 2 lesson (asymmetric TP/SL inflates score vs realized), realistic 30-day paper DD scales to ~2-3x the median trade %, plus squeeze-trap clustering during BTC downtrends → 8-10% range.
compare_to_existing: E148 pc_pump_global_short_high_long (pricechange channel, long, best=+0.350, trig=988) — direct sibling, same novel_dim logic ported across channels; secondary triangulation against E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long for left-tail behavior since both are crowded-short long-squeeze plays.
